title: Jamaican Jerk Chicken with Grilled Plantain, Garbanzo-Lime Side & Mango Salsa
description: Grilled jerk chicken with allspice and Scotch bonnet heat, caramelized plantains, a quick garbanzo-lime side, and fresh mango salsa. Low sat fat, high protein.

ingredients.name: boneless skinless chicken thighs or breasts
Scotch bonnet or habanero pepper, seeds removed for less heat
canned garbanzo beans, drained and rinsed

instructions.text: Make the jerk marinade: blend scallions, garlic, ginger, Scotch bonnet, allspice, thyme, cinnamon, nutmeg, soy sauce, lime juice, brown sugar, and avocado oil in a food processor to a thick paste, adding a splash of water if needed. Rub all over chicken, cover, and refrigerate at least 1 hour -- ideally 4–8 hours or overnight.
Make the mango salsa: combine mango, tomato (if using), red onion, jalapeño, cilantro, and lime juice. Toss gently, season with salt, and let sit at room temp while you grill so the flavors meld.

Make the garbanzo side: warm olive oil in a small pan over medium heat. Add minced garlic and cook 30 seconds. Add garbanzo beans and cumin, stir to coat, and heat through 3–4 minutes. Off heat, stir in lime juice and cilantro. Season with salt.
Peel plantains and slice on a diagonal into 3/4-inch thick pieces. Toss with avocado oil.
Preheat grill to medium-high (~400°F). Grill chicken 5–7 minutes per side until internal temp hits 165°F and you have good char. Rest 5 minutes before slicing.
Grill plantain slices alongside or after chicken, about 3–4 minutes per side, until caramelized and golden with grill marks.
Slice chicken and plate with plantains and garbanzo side. Top chicken generously with mango salsa.

notes:
Heat level: one Scotch bonnet with seeds removed is moderate. Leave seeds in for real heat, or use half a pepper for milder.

Ripe plantains are key -- look for yellow with heavy black spotting. Green plantains won't caramelize the same way.

The garbanzo side and mango salsa can both be made while the chicken marinates.
